definition: The ECB is structurally more rate-differential-sensitive than the Fed due to ~70% bank intermediation dependency versus ~30% in the US. When bank lending standards tighten, the ECB loses its primary transmission channel while the Fed retains capital market transmission. Current bank lending pass-through at 60-70% versus historical 85-90%.
evidence: ~70% bank intermediation for ECB vs. ~30% for US
pair_3 called this genuinely useful structural insight for EUR/USD analysis
Explains ECB FX sensitivity through transmission channel dependency, not trade exposure
open_questions: Is the ECB developing capital market alternatives that could reduce bank dependency?
How does this structural difference affect EUR/USD response to symmetric rate changes?
Does the pass-through degradation (85-90% → 60-70%) indicate secular decline or cyclical compression?
